news.summary: In Las Vegas, Rimini Street, Inc. (Nasdaq: RMNI), a prominent enterprise software support provider, announced a collaboration with Apsen Farmacêutica, a Brazilian pharmaceutical company, at ServiceNow’s Knowledge 2025 event. Rimini Street, known for offering third-party support for Oracle, SAP, and VMware software, joined forces with ServiceNow to introduce an innovative approach. This partnership signifies Apsen Farmacêutica's move towards a cutting-edge operational vision.
news.headline: Rimini Street and ServiceNow partner with Apsen Farmacêutica for workflow automation. Announcement made at Knowledge 2025. Brazilian pharmaceutical manufacturer involved.

news.summary: In Portland, Oregon, EFM, a company specializing in forest investment and management, has recently completed a long-term agreement with Microsoft. This deal involves a fund investment that guarantees Microsoft's acquisition of a maximum of 3 million nature-based carbon removal credits from EFM's investment platform. As part of the agreement, there is a commitment to provide up to 700,000 credits until 2035. These credits will be sourced from a newly obtained property located in the Olympic Peninsula of Washington State. EFM is currently converting this property to a climate-friendly forest management site.
news.headline: EFM partners with Microsoft for carbon removals, with deal securing access to 3 million credits, including 700,000 by 2035.

news.summary: A recent report on the China Construction Market projects significant growth in various sectors. The industry experienced a 3.8% growth in 2024 and is anticipated to sustain this momentum with a 3.2% growth in 2025. The report covers key sectors such as Commercial, Industrial, Infrastructure, Energy and Utilities, Institutional, and Residential markets. The analysis extends up to 2029 (Q1 2025), offering insights into market trends and forecasts. The findings showcase a positive trajectory for the Chinese construction industry, highlighting ongoing developments and opportunities for growth.
news.headline: By 2025, China's construction industry is expected to grow by 3.2% in real terms, with an AAGR of 4.1% projected for 2026-2029.

news.summary: Bybit exchange has recovered its liquidity to pre-hack levels within 30 days of the February 2025 attack that drained $1.5 billion in funds. Bitcoin's 1% market depth has returned to around $13 million per day in March 2025. Altcoin liquidity on the exchange has rebounded to about 80% of pre-hack levels. The recovery has been slower for altcoins due to the risk-off market environment post-hack. Bybit's trading volumes are still in recovery, reflecting broader market trends amid ongoing macroeconomic uncertainty. The hack was caused by a compromised device from SafeWallet, the firm behind the wallet custody solution used by Bybit. CEO Ben Zhou assured investors that the exchange was solvent, and the industry came together to support Bybit post-hack. Other exchanges provided assistance and froze stolen funds.
news.headline: Bybit restores liquidity after $1.5B hack in February 2025; BTC liquidity at $13M/day. Altcoin levels rebounded to 80%. No shift in crypto security despite April hacks. CEO ensures solvency post-hack, industry support given. Cryptocurrency exchanges' fund handling scrutinized.

news.headline: Wall Street mixed ahead of Fed meeting, Alphabet sinks 7%. Dow leads, Nasdaq lags. Gas prices surge 5% to $3.640/MMBtu.

news.summary: A recent report on the construction market in Thailand predicts a 5.9% growth in real terms in 2025. This growth is attributed to investments in tourism and digital infrastructure, as well as an uptick in export activities. The report, available on ResearchAndMarkets.com, covers sectors such as commercial, industrial, infrastructure, energy and utilities, institutional, and residential markets up to 2029. This positive outlook underscores the potential for expansion and development within the Thai construction industry.
news.headline: Thailand construction industry output to grow by 5.9% in 2025, fueled by tourism, digital infrastructure investments.

news.summary: A report on the Portugal Construction Market, recently added to ResearchAndMarkets.com, projects the industry to see a 2.5% growth in 2025. This growth will be primarily driven by increased building permits and export activities. Additionally, investments in transport and energy projects are expected to contribute to this positive trend. The report provides detailed analysis by sector, including Commercial, Industrial, Infrastructure, Energy and Utilities, Institutional, and Residential markets. The forecast covers trends and market size analysis up to 2029 (H1), offering valuable insights for stakeholders in the construction industry.
news.headline: Portugal's construction industry to grow by 2.5% in 2025, with an AAGR of 1.7% expected during 2026-2029.

news.summary: The news article highlights the recent rallies in the crypto market, with Bitcoin (BTC) reaching $97,000 (+3%), Ethereum (ETH) at $1,840 (+2%), and Solana (SOL) at $147 (+2%). Top gainers include Litecoin (LTC), Stacks (STX), WILD (WLD), Fetch.ai (FET), and Quant (QNT). BTC ETFs faced a decline of $86 million, while ETH ETFs dropped by $18 million. The market was buoyed by US-China trade talks, with BTC dominance surpassing 65%. New Hampshire became the first state to establish a crypto reserve, while the UK Treasury ruled out a BTC reserve. Thumzup planned to sell $500 million stock to buy BTC, while SOL Strategies purchased $18.25 million worth of SOL. Citi and SDX aimed to tokenize the pre-IPO market. Global stablecoin supply rose by 14% in 2025, Tether planned to introduce USDT on Kaia, and WLFI was set to airdrop stablecoin on the ETH mainnet. Michael Saylor expressed AI's intent to buy BTC, Bitwise applied for a NEAR ETF, Strike introduced BTC-backed lending, and Brian Armstrong's startup was valued at $810 million. CZ looked to apply for a pardon in the US, and Bhutan started accepting crypto payments for tourists. Bessent stated opposition to the Fed issuing a CBDC. FOMO HOUR podcast covers these and other significant topics daily, hosted by Farokh, Mando, and Tyler, streaming on YouTube and X from Monday to Friday. The article provides links for further information on the topics discussed and the hosts' social media profiles.
news.headline: Crypto surges as US-China trade talks start and FOMC decision looms. BTC at $97k (+3%), ETH at $1840 (+2%), SOL at $147 (+2%). Top Gainers: LTC, STX, WLD, FET, QNT. BTC ETFs - $86mn, ETH ETFs - $18mn. BTC dominance at 65%. New Hampshire pioneers crypto reserve. UK Treasury rejects BTC reserves. Thumzup to trade $500m stocks for BTC. SOL Strategies buys $18.25m SOL. Citi, SDX plan pre-IPO tokenisation. Global stablecoin supply up 14% in 2025. Tether to use USDT on Kaia. WLFI airdrops stablecoin on ETH. AI to invest in BTC: Saylor. Bitwise files NEAR ETF. Strike launches BTC-backed lending. Armstrong’s startup valued at $810m. CZ seeks pardon in the US. Bhutan adopts crypto for tourist payments. No CBDC wanted by Bessent.

news.summary: On May 7, 2025, the REAL ID Act, signed into law by President George W. Bush almost two decades ago, is now in effect, aiming to bolster national security and safeguard election integrity. This long-anticipated implementation comes as a crucial step in ensuring the authenticity of identification documents.
news.headline: REAL ID Act goes into effect after 20 years, enhancing national security. Will protect elections integrity.

news.summary: Shares of Charles River Laboratories International Inc (NYSE: CRL) saw a surge in trading following the release of their impressive first-quarter 2025 earnings. The life science tools company reported revenue of $984.2 million, surpassing the expected $941.97 million by 2.7%. Despite a 2.7% decrease in revenue compared to the previous year, the company's operating margin dropped to 7.6% from 12.5%, attributed to lower revenue and increased amortization expenses. However, with adjustments, the operating margin rose to 19.1% from 18.5%, driven by cost-saving efforts from restructuring. Their adjusted EPS stood at $2.34, up from $2.27 in the previous year, beating the consensus estimate of $2.08. Specifically, revenue for the Research Models and Services (RMS) segment landed at $213.1 million, marking a 3.5% decrease year over year. The positive earnings report indicates a strong performance despite challenges, with more details available on financial news site Benzinga.com.
news.headline: Charles River Laboratories Q1 earnings beat consensus with revenue at $984.2 million, surpassing $941.97 million estimate. Stock rises on signs of demand stabilization, with adjusted EPS of $2.34 exceeding $2.08 estimate.
